title: Benefits of Exercise for Students
context: Your school magazine is publishing a special issue on healthy living.
task: Write a short article for the school magazine explaining why exercise is important for students and how they can incorporate physical activity into their daily routine.
suggestedPoints: Explain how exercise improves concentration and grades
Discuss mental health benefits of physical activity
Suggest simple exercises during study breaks
Recommend joining school sports teams
Encourage readers to start small and stay consistent
instructions: Give your article a title. Write as a senior student sharing advice.

title: How Social Media Affects Teenagers
context: Your school's English Society is publishing a newsletter about the impact of social media on young people.
task: Write a short article discussing both the positive and negative effects of social media on teenagers.
suggestedPoints: Acknowledge that most teenagers use social media daily
Discuss positive aspects: staying connected, learning opportunities
Address negative aspects: cyberbullying, comparison, screen time
Suggest healthy usage habits
Conclude with a balanced perspective
instructions: Give your article an engaging title. Write in a formal but accessible style.

title: Review of a Local Restaurant
context: You visited a newly opened restaurant in your neighbourhood last weekend.
task: Write a blog post sharing your dining experience. Describe the atmosphere, food quality, service, and whether you would recommend it to others.
suggestedPoints: Describe the restaurant's location and decor
Comment on the food and presentation
Describe the service and staff attitude
Mention the price and value for money
Give your overall rating and recommendation
instructions: Write as a blog post with a catchy title. Use informal language.

title: School Exchange Programme Inquiry
context: You are a Form 6 student at Hong Kong Heights Secondary School. Your school is organising acultural exchange programme with a school in the UK.
task: Write an email to the UK school's programme coordinator asking for details about the exchange. You should introduce yourself, express interest, and ask about accommodation, daily schedule, and costs.
suggestedPoints: Introduce yourself and your school
Express enthusiasm for the programme
Ask about accommodation arrangements
Inquire about daily schedule
Ask about programme costs and what is included
instructions: Sign your email with the name Chris Wong. Do not write any addresses.

title: Complaint About Smartphone Purchase
context: You recently bought a smartphone from an online store, but it arrived with defects.
task: Write a formal letter of complaint to the customer service manager. Describe the problem, explain how it affects you, and state what you expect the company to do.
suggestedPoints: Describe when and where you bought the phone
Explain the defects (screen flickering, battery overheating)
State that this is unacceptable for a new device
Request a replacement or full refund
Set a reasonable deadline for response
instructions: Sign your letter with the name Chris Wong. Do not write any addresses.

title: Volunteer Application Letter
context: You saw an advertisement for summer volunteer positions at the Hong Kong Youth Arts Festival.
task: Write a letter applying for the volunteer position. Explain why you are interested, what skills you can contribute, and your relevant experience.
suggestedPoints: State which position you are applying for
Explain your interest in arts and culture
Describe any relevant experience (school clubs, previous volunteering)
Highlight your teamwork and communication skills
Express enthusiasm and willingness to learn
instructions: Sign your letter with the name Chris Wong. Address it to the Volunteer Coordinator.

title: Letter to Editor About Public Transport
context: You have noticed that public transport in your district has become increasingly unreliable.
task: Write a letter to the editor of the South China Morning Post expressing your concerns about public transport and suggesting improvements.
suggestedPoints: Describe specific problems (delays, overcrowding, maintenance)
Explain how these issues affect residents' daily lives
Suggest concrete improvements (more buses, better scheduling)
Acknowledge any recent positive changes
End with a constructive call for action
instructions: Sign your letter with the name Chris Wong. Address it to The Editor.

title: Speech on Reducing Plastic Waste
context: Your school's Green Club has asked you to give a short speech during morning assembly about reducing plastic waste on campus.
task: Write a speech encouraging your fellow students to reduce plastic waste. Explain why it matters and suggest practical steps everyone can take.
suggestedPoints: Open with a striking fact about plastic pollution
Explain how plastic waste affects Hong Kong's marine environment
Suggest bringing reusable bottles and containers
Propose a plastic-free day each month
End with a call to action
instructions: Start with 'Good morning, Principal, teachers, and fellow students.' Do not write your name.

title: The Importance of Digital Literacy
context: Your class has been asked to prepare a short presentation for a school assembly on digital literacy.
task: Write a speech explaining why digital literacy is important in today's world and how students can improve their digital skills.
suggestedPoints: Define digital literacy beyond just using social media
Explain how digital skills are essential for future careers
Discuss online safety and responsible internet use
Suggest resources for learning coding and digital tools
Encourage students to use technology creatively
instructions: Start with 'Good morning, everyone.' Do not write your name.

title: Should Hong Kong Ban Single-Use Plastics?
context: You are a reporter for the Hong Kong Post, a student newspaper. The government is considering a ban on single-use plastics, and your editor has asked you to write a feature article exploring both sides of the debate.
task: Write an article discussing the arguments for and against banning single-use plastics in Hong Kong. Consider environmental, economic, and social perspectives, and state your own view.
suggestedPoints: Describe the current plastic waste problem in Hong Kong
Present environmental arguments for a ban
Discuss economic concerns for businesses and consumers
Consider alternatives and their feasibility
State your own reasoned position
instructions: Give your article a headline. Write as a student journalist.

title: The Rise of E-Sports in Hong Kong
context: Your school's English newspaper is featuring a series on emerging trends in youth culture. The editor has asked you to write about the growing popularity of e-sports among Hong Kong youth.
task: Write an article discussing the rise of e-sports in Hong Kong. Consider its impact on young people, the debate over whether it should be considered a sport, and its potential as a career path.
suggestedPoints: Describe how e-sports have grown in popularity in Hong Kong
Present arguments for recognising e-sports as legitimate competition
Discuss concerns about screen time and sedentary lifestyles
Explore career opportunities in the e-sports industry
Offer a balanced conclusion
instructions: Give your article an engaging headline. Write for a student audience.

title: A Week Without My Smartphone
context: You recently challenged yourself to spend one week without your smartphone. Your friend has invited you to write a guest blog post on their lifestyle blog about the experience.
task: Write a blog post describing your week without a smartphone. Reflect on what you learned, what you struggled with, and whether you would recommend the challenge to others.
suggestedPoints: Describe how you felt before starting the challenge
Share specific moments of difficulty and discovery
Explain changes in your daily routine
Reflect on your relationship with technology
Give honest advice to readers considering the challenge
instructions: Write in a conversational, engaging style. Give your post a catchy title.

title: Proposal for School Mental Health Programme
context: As the Chairperson of the Student Council, you believe that your school needs a more comprehensive mental health support system for students.
task: Write a formal letter to the Principal proposing a school mental health programme. Explain why it is needed, what it should include, and how it could be implemented.
suggestedPoints: Highlight the growing mental health challenges students face
Propose specific initiatives (counselling, peer support, workshops)
Explain how the programme would benefit the school community
Suggest how to implement it with minimal disruption
Offer to assist with planning and promotion
instructions: Sign your letter as Chris Wong, Chairperson of the Student Council. Use formal language.

title: Proposal for a School Entrepreneurship Club
context: You believe your school should establish an Entrepreneurship Club to help students develop business and leadership skills.
task: Write a proposal to the Principal outlining a plan for a new Entrepreneurship Club. Explain the club's objectives, activities, resource needs, and expected benefits.
suggestedPoints: Explain why entrepreneurship education matters for students
Propose specific club activities (workshops, competitions, mentorship)
Outline resource requirements (space, budget, teacher supervision)
Describe how the club would be organised
Highlight expected benefits for students and the school
instructions: Format your proposal with clear sections. Use persuasive but professional language.

title: Report on Campus Recycling Initiative
context: The Green Committee of your school launched a campus recycling initiative three months ago. As the committee secretary, you have been asked to evaluate its effectiveness.
task: Write a report evaluating the recycling initiative. Include data on participation rates, challenges encountered, and recommendations for improvement.
suggestedPoints: Summarise the initiative's objectives and timeline
Present participation data and observed trends
Identify challenges (contamination, lack of awareness)
Compare with similar programmes in other Hong Kong schools
Make specific recommendations for improvement
instructions: Write a formal report with sections and headings. Sign as Chris Wong, Green Committee Secretary.

title: Survey Results: Student Stress Levels Before Exams
context: The Student Council conducted a survey on student stress levels during the examination period. As the council's vice-chairperson, you need to present the findings.
task: Write a report analysing the survey results on student stress before exams. Identify the main causes of stress, the coping strategies students use, and recommend ways the school can provide better support.
suggestedPoints: Summarise the survey methodology and sample size
Present key findings on stress causes (academic pressure, time management)
Analyse coping strategies students currently use
Identify gaps in school support services
Recommend concrete actions the school should take
instructions: Use formal report format with section headings. Present data clearly.

title: Review of an Online Learning Platform
context: Your school is considering subscribing to an online learning platform for exam revision. As the senior prefect, you have been asked to try the platform and write a review.
task: Write a review of an online learning platform of your choice. Evaluate its content quality, ease of use, value for money, and suitability for DSE preparation.
suggestedPoints: Describe the platform's features and content
Assess the quality of learning materials for DSE subjects
Evaluate the user interface and mobile accessibility
Compare the cost with the educational value provided
Give a clear recommendation and rating
instructions: Write a balanced, well-structured review. Give it a title and a star rating (1-5).

title: The Value of Learning a Third Language
context: You have been invited to speak at the Hong Kong Youth Language Forum about multilingual education in Hong Kong schools.
task: Write a speech arguing that Hong Kong students should be encouraged to learn a third language beyond English and Cantonese. Discuss the academic, professional, and cultural benefits.
suggestedPoints: Open with your personal experience of learning a language
Explain cognitive benefits of multilingualism
Discuss career advantages in Hong Kong's global economy
Address practical challenges and how to overcome them
Call on schools to offer more language options
instructions: Open your speech with an engaging hook. Write for an audience of educators and students. Do not use real personal details.

title: One Small Act of Kindness
context: Your school magazine is running a short story competition on the theme 'One Small Act of Kindness Can Change a Life.'
task: Write a story based on this theme. Your story should have a clear plot, setting, and characters, and convey how a simple act of kindness had a significant impact.
suggestedPoints: Establish the setting and introduce the main character
Describe the situation that calls for kindness
Show the act of kindness and the immediate reaction
Reveal the longer-term impact on the characters involved
End with a meaningful reflection
instructions: Give your story a title. Use descriptive language to bring the narrative to life.

title: The Last Traditional Shop on the Street
context: A local literary magazine is running a writing competition on the theme of Hong Kong's disappearing traditional culture.
task: Write a short story about the last traditional shop on a street that is being transformed by modern development. Explore themes of change, memory, and belonging.
suggestedPoints: Establish the setting and describe the traditional shop vividly
Introduce the shop owner and their connection to the community
Show the contrast between old and new Hong Kong
Develop a conflict or turning point
End with a moment of reflection or resolution
instructions: Give your story a title. Use sensory details to bring the setting to life.

title: School Sports Day Announcement
context: You are a member of the Sports Committee at your school. The school is holding its annual Sports Day next Friday.
task: Write a notice to all students informing them about the Sports Day. Include the date, venue, events available, and registration details.
suggestedPoints: State the date, time and venue of Sports Day
List at least three events available
Explain how students can register
Mention any important rules or requirements
Include a deadline for registration
instructions: Write a formal notice. Include a heading, date, and your name as Secretary of the Sports Committee.

title: Class Trip Planning Memo
context: You are the class representative of Form 5B. Your homeroom teacher has asked you to circulate a memo to collect suggestions for the upcoming class trip.
task: Write a memo to your classmates asking for their suggestions for the class trip. Include the purpose of the trip, deadline for suggestions, and what kind of information you need.
suggestedPoints: State the purpose of the memo clearly
Mention the proposed date and destination for the trip
Ask for suggestions on activities, food, and transportation
Set a clear deadline for responses
Provide your contact information for replies
instructions: Write as a memo. Use 'To:', 'From:', 'Date:', and 'Subject:' headers. Sign as the Class Representative.

title: Library Membership Application
context: You want to join the Hong Kong Public Library's student membership programme. You need to complete an application form.
task: Complete the application form for the Hong Kong Public Library student membership. Fill in your personal details, student information, and select your preferred library branch.
suggestedPoints: Provide complete personal details (name, date of birth, contact)
State your school name and student status
Choose a library branch and justify your choice
Indicate your reading interests and preferred book categories
Confirm your agreement to library rules and regulations
instructions: Complete the form as if you are a real student. Use your own name or 'Chris Wong'. Sign at the bottom.

title: First Day at a New School
context: You have just transferred to a new secondary school in Hong Kong. Write about your first day.
task: Write a diary entry about your experiences on the first day at your new school. Describe what happened, how you felt, and what you learned from the experience.
suggestedPoints: Describe your feelings before arriving at the new school
Record at least two specific events that happened during the day
Mention someone you met and how the interaction went
Reflect on how the day compared to your expectations
Express your hopes or worries about fitting in
instructions: Write as a personal diary entry with a date at the top. Use first-person narrative and an informal, reflective tone.

title: Requesting a School Exchange Visit
context: You are the president of the English Society at your school. You want to arrange an exchange visit with a school in Singapore to improve students' English skills.
task: Write an email to the English Department head of a partner school in Singapore proposing an exchange visit. Explain the purpose, suggest dates, and outline what you hope to achieve.
suggestedPoints: Introduce yourself and your school
Explain the purpose of the exchange visit
Suggest specific dates or a time frame
Outline proposed activities (cultural tours, joint classes, etc.)
Request a response and offer to discuss further
instructions: Write a formal email. Use 'Dear [Name]' and 'Yours sincerely,'. Sign as Chris Wong, President of the English Society.

title: Letter to the Editor — Traffic Congestion
context: Traffic congestion on the M1 highway during rush hours has become a serious problem in your district.
task: Write a letter to the editor of the South China Morning Post commenting on the traffic problem and suggesting possible solutions.
suggestedPoints: Describe the current traffic situation in your district
Explain the impact on residents' daily lives
Propose at least two practical solutions
Acknowledge any efforts the government has already made
End with a call for collective action
instructions: Write a formal letter to the editor. Sign as Chris Wong. Do not write any addresses.

title: Is AI a Threat or a Tool for Students?
context: Your school newspaper is running a series on technology and education. The editor has asked you to write an article exploring how artificial intelligence is affecting students' learning.
task: Write an article discussing whether AI is a threat to students' learning or a valuable tool. Consider both sides of the argument and state your own view.
suggestedPoints: Describe how AI tools are being used by students today
Present arguments that AI enhances learning
Present arguments that AI undermines learning
Discuss the importance of responsible use
State your own balanced position
instructions: Give your article a compelling headline. Write for a student audience with a mix of formal and accessible language.

title: The Decline of Traditional Markets in Hong Kong
context: Your local district magazine is publishing an issue on the changing landscape of Hong Kong. The editor has asked you to write about the impact of supermarkets and online shopping on traditional wet markets.
task: Write an article discussing why traditional markets are declining and whether this is a loss worth mourning or a natural evolution of commerce.
suggestedPoints: Describe the role of traditional markets in Hong Kong culture
Explain the factors contributing to their decline
Discuss the benefits of modern alternatives
Argue for preserving cultural heritage
Offer a conclusion that balances tradition and progress
instructions: Give your article a catchy title. Write in a tone suitable for a community magazine.

title: My Experience Volunteering at a Nursing Home
context: You recently volunteered at a nursing home in your district for a weekend. Your friend's community blog is looking for personal stories about volunteering.
task: Write a blog post sharing your experience volunteering at a nursing home. Describe what you did, how it made you feel, and what you learned about elderly care in Hong Kong.
suggestedPoints: Set the scene — describe the nursing home and your first impressions
Share a specific moment or interaction that stood out
Reflect on what you learned about the elderly
Discuss the broader issue of elderly care in Hong Kong
Encourage readers to consider volunteering
instructions: Write in a warm, personal tone. End with a call to action for readers to get involved.

title: Surviving the DSE Pressure Cooker
context: You are a Form 6 student who has just completed your mock exams. A youth lifestyle website is asking students to write about managing exam stress.
task: Write a blog post sharing your strategies for dealing with DSE exam pressure. Be honest about the challenges and offer practical advice to younger students.
suggestedPoints: Describe the pressure of DSE preparation from your perspective
Share at least three specific coping strategies you've used
Discuss the importance of balancing study and rest
Address the mental health aspect of exam preparation
End with encouragement for students yet to face the exams
instructions: Write in a relatable, conversational tone. Use headings to break up your post.

title: Letter to the Chief Executive on Youth Unemployment
context: You are the chairperson of a youth advocacy group. You want to write to the Chief Executive of the HKSAR expressing concern about rising youth unemployment in Hong Kong.
task: Write a formal letter to the Chief Executive outlining the youth unemployment problem, its causes, and specific policy recommendations to address it.
suggestedPoints: State the current youth unemployment situation with data
Identify key causes (skills mismatch, economic factors, etc.)
Propose specific policy interventions
Reference successful models from other countries
Request a meeting to discuss further
instructions: Write in a formal, respectful but firm tone. Sign as Chairperson of the Youth Advocacy Group.

title: Proposal for a Peer Tutoring Programme
context: You believe your school should establish a peer tutoring programme where senior students help junior students with academic subjects.
task: Write a proposal to the Principal recommending the establishment of a peer tutoring programme. Outline the benefits, structure, and implementation plan.
suggestedPoints: Explain the academic challenges students face
Describe how peer tutoring would work in practice
Outline the benefits for both tutors and tutees
Propose a pilot programme structure
Address potential concerns (scheduling, training)
instructions: Use clear headings for each section. Maintain a persuasive but professional tone.

title: Report on Student Sleep Habits
context: The Student Council conducted a survey on student sleep habits and found that 70% of students get less than six hours of sleep per night. You have been asked to write a report on the findings.
task: Write a report presenting the survey findings on student sleep habits, analysing the causes, and recommending actions the school should take.
suggestedPoints: Summarise the survey methodology and key findings
Analyse the main causes of sleep deprivation among students
Discuss the impact on academic performance and health
Compare with sleep research recommendations for teenagers
Recommend specific interventions the school can implement
instructions: Use formal report format with clear section headings. Include relevant statistics and data.

title: The Power of Reading in the Digital Age
context: You have been invited to speak at your school's annual Reading Week assembly on the importance of reading books in an era dominated by social media and short-form content.
task: Write a speech arguing for the continued importance of reading books, especially among young people in Hong Kong's digital age.
suggestedPoints: Open with a compelling hook about reading habits
Discuss the benefits of deep reading vs. skimming
Address the challenge of competing digital distractions
Share personal or observed examples of reading's impact
End with an inspiring call to make reading a daily habit
instructions: Write for oral delivery — use rhetorical devices, direct address, and a persuasive tone. Do not sign your name.

title: Why Hong Kong Needs More Green Spaces
context: You are speaking at a youth environmental forum about urban planning and the need for more parks and green spaces in Hong Kong.
task: Write a speech arguing for increased investment in green spaces in Hong Kong's urban landscape.
suggestedPoints: Describe the current state of green spaces in Hong Kong
Explain the environmental benefits (air quality, heat island effect)
Discuss the mental health benefits for residents
Address the challenge of limited land
Propose creative solutions (rooftop gardens, vertical parks)
instructions: Use a passionate but measured tone. Include rhetorical questions and direct appeals to the audience.

title: The Last Tram Ride
context: A local literary magazine is running a writing competition on the theme of 'changes in Hong Kong.'
task: Write a short story about a character experiencing a meaningful moment on the last ride of a Hong Kong tram before it is decommissioned for an upgrade.
suggestedPoints: Establish the setting — describe the tram and its atmosphere
Introduce the protagonist and their connection to the tram
Show interactions with other passengers during the ride
Build to a moment of reflection or revelation
End with a resonant image or thought
instructions: Give your story a title. Use vivid sensory details and a reflective tone.

title: Review of a Local DSE Prep Course
context: Your school's alumni magazine is asking former students to review educational resources that helped them prepare for the DSE.
task: Write a review of a DSE preparation course or resource you used. Evaluate its effectiveness, value for money, and suitability for different types of students.
suggestedPoints: Describe the course/resource and what it covers
Evaluate the quality of teaching and materials
Discuss who would benefit most from it
Compare it with other options available
Give a clear recommendation with justification
instructions: Write a balanced, professional review. Include a star rating (1-5) and a clear verdict.

title: The NEET Generation in Hong Kong
context: Your school newspaper is publishing a special edition on youth issues in Hong Kong. The editor has asked you to write about the growing number of young people who are Not in Education, Employment, or Training (NEET).
task: Write an article examining the phenomenon of NEET youth in Hong Kong, exploring the causes and potential solutions.
suggestedPoints: Define what NEET means and its prevalence in Hong Kong
Explore the social and economic factors behind the trend
Discuss the impact on individuals and society
Examine government and community responses
Offer your perspective on what more can be done
instructions: Give your article a provocative headline. Write for a general audience with a balance of facts and opinion.

title: Proposal for a Mental Health Awareness Week
context: Following a survey showing increasing stress levels among students, the Student Union has asked you to propose a Mental Health Awareness Week for your school.
task: Write a proposal to the school administration outlining a plan for a Mental Health Awareness Week, including activities, speakers, and expected outcomes.
suggestedPoints: Explain why mental health awareness is needed in schools
Propose specific activities (workshops, talks, peer support)
Suggest potential speakers or partners
Outline a realistic timetable for the week
Address budget and resource considerations
instructions: Use clear headings and a professional tone. Make a compelling case for the proposal's importance.

title: Why We Should Learn Cantonese History
context: You have been asked to give a speech at a Chinese Cultural Heritage Day event at your school about the importance of understanding local history.
task: Write a speech arguing that Hong Kong students should learn more about Cantonese and local history to strengthen their cultural identity.
suggestedPoints: Open with a personal anecdote about discovering local history
Explain why local history is often overlooked in education
Discuss the value of cultural identity for young people
Address the concern that it takes time away from core subjects
Call on the school and government to integrate local history into the curriculum
instructions: Write for a mixed audience of students, teachers, and community members. Use an engaging, persuasive tone.

title: The Forgotten Letter
context: A local literary magazine is running a competition on the theme 'Messages That Changed Everything.'
task: Write a short story about a character who discovers a forgotten letter that reveals a family secret and changes their understanding of their past.
suggestedPoints: Set the scene — describe where the letter is found
Introduce the protagonist and their family dynamics
Reveal the content of the letter gradually
Show the emotional impact on the protagonist
End with a resolution that leaves room for reflection
instructions: Give your story a title. Use a suspenseful tone that builds to an emotional climax.

title: Report on the Impact of Short-Form Video Apps on Students
context: The school library committee has commissioned a report on how short-form video apps (TikTok, Instagram Reels, YouTube Shorts) are affecting students' study habits and attention spans.
task: Write a report analysing the impact of short-form video apps on students' learning and behaviour, based on surveys and research findings.
suggestedPoints: Summarise current usage patterns among students
Present research findings on attention span and study habits
Discuss both positive and negative effects
Compare with findings from other studies
Recommend guidelines for healthy digital consumption
instructions: Write a formal report with clear sections. Use data and citations where appropriate.

title: The Case for Mandatory Financial Literacy in Schools
context: Your school's alumni magazine is running a series on education reform. The editor has asked you to write an article advocating for financial literacy education in secondary schools.
task: Write an article arguing that Hong Kong secondary schools should make financial literacy a mandatory part of the curriculum.
suggestedPoints: Describe the current gap in financial education
Explain why financial literacy matters for young people
Discuss what topics should be covered (budgeting, investing, debt)
Address potential objections (curriculum overload)
Conclude with a strong call for curriculum reform
instructions: Give your article a thought-provoking headline. Write for an adult audience of parents and educators.

title: Why We Need to Protect Our Food Heritage
context: You are speaking at a community forum on cultural preservation in Hong Kong about the threat facing traditional food shops and recipes.
task: Write a speech arguing for the preservation of Hong Kong's food heritage in the face of modernisation and gentrification.
suggestedPoints: Open with a vivid description of a traditional food experience
Explain what is being lost (shops, recipes, techniques)
Discuss the cultural and social value of food heritage
Propose specific preservation measures
End with an emotional appeal to the community
instructions: Write for a mixed audience of residents and policymakers. Use evocative language and rhetorical devices.

title: Review of a Hong Kong Cultural Festival
context: You attended the Hong Kong Arts Festival last weekend. A local lifestyle website is asking readers to review cultural events.
task: Write a review of a cultural event or festival you attended. Evaluate the quality of the programme, accessibility, and overall experience.
suggestedPoints: Describe the event and its significance
Evaluate the quality of performances or exhibitions
Discuss the organisation and attendee experience
Compare with similar events locally or internationally
Give a clear recommendation and rating
instructions: Write a balanced review with both praise and constructive criticism. Include a star rating.

title: Living on a Budget as a DSE Student
context: You are a DSE student who has learned to manage your finances carefully. A youth finance blog is asking students to share their money management tips.
task: Write a blog post sharing your experience of living on a budget as a secondary student and offering practical money-saving tips for your peers.
suggestedPoints: Describe your monthly budget and income sources
Share specific strategies for saving money
Discuss the challenges of balancing study and part-time work
Offer advice on distinguishing wants from needs
End with encouragement for financially conscious living
instructions: Write in a friendly, relatable tone. Use bullet points or numbered lists for tips.

title: Letter to a Friend About Choosing a University Major
context: Your friend is struggling to decide which university major to pursue. You have recently gone through the application process and want to share your experience.
task: Write a letter to your friend offering advice on choosing a university major. Share your own experience, discuss factors to consider, and encourage them to make their own decision.
suggestedPoints: Acknowledge your friend's dilemma with empathy
Share your own decision-making process
Discuss factors to consider (passion, career prospects, skills)
Advise on how to research different majors
Encourage them to trust their instincts
instructions: Write in a warm, supportive tone appropriate for a letter to a close friend.

title: The Hidden Cost of Fast Fashion
context: Your school's environmental club is publishing a newsletter on sustainable living. The editor has asked you to write about the environmental impact of fast fashion.
task: Write an article exposing the environmental and social costs of fast fashion and advocating for more sustainable clothing choices.
suggestedPoints: Describe the rise of fast fashion and its appeal to young people
Explain the environmental impact (water pollution, waste)
Discuss the labour conditions in garment factories
Propose sustainable alternatives (thrifting, capsule wardrobes)
Conclude with a call for conscious consumerism
instructions: Give your article a striking headline. Write for a young, socially-conscious audience.

title: Report on the Effectiveness of Online Learning
context: The school board has asked you to write a report evaluating the effectiveness of online learning during the recent pandemic period.
task: Write a report assessing how well online learning worked for students, teachers, and the school community, and making recommendations for future hybrid models.
suggestedPoints: Summarise the online learning experience during the pandemic
Present student and teacher feedback
Analyse the strengths and weaknesses of online delivery
Compare with traditional classroom learning
Recommend a hybrid model for future use
instructions: Write a formal report with clear sections. Use an objective, analytical tone.

title: The Courage to Be Different
context: You have been invited to speak at your school's graduation ceremony on the theme of embracing individuality in a conformist society.
task: Write a graduation speech encouraging students to stay true to themselves and pursue their unique passions, even when it means going against the crowd.
suggestedPoints: Open with a personal story about standing out
Discuss the pressure to conform in Hong Kong's competitive culture
Share examples of people who succeeded by being different
Address the fear of failure and judgment
End with an inspiring call to embrace individuality
instructions: Write for a graduation audience. Use an uplifting, motivational tone with rhetorical devices.

title: The Last Page of the Diary
context: A school literary magazine is running a competition on the theme 'Stories in Small Things.'
task: Write a short story about a student who finds their grandmother's old diary and discovers a hidden chapter that changes their understanding of their family.
suggestedPoints: Describe the discovery of the diary in an unexpected place
Show the student's initial curiosity turning to deeper interest
Reveal a pivotal entry that changes everything
Explore the emotional impact on the student
End with a moment of connection across generations
instructions: Give your story a title. Use a reflective, intimate tone with strong emotional resonance.

title: Proposal for a School Mental Health First Aid Training
context: After several students experienced anxiety attacks during exam season, the Student Welfare Committee has asked you to propose a Mental Health First Aid training programme.
task: Write a proposal to the school administration recommending a Mental Health First Aid training programme for students and staff.
suggestedPoints: Explain the growing mental health crisis among youth
Describe what Mental Health First Aid training involves
Outline the benefits for students and staff
Propose a pilot programme with timeline and budget
Address potential concerns and provide reassurance
instructions: Use a professional, evidence-based tone. Cite relevant statistics and research.

title: The Gig Economy and Young Workers in Hong Kong
context: Your school's economics club is publishing a journal on contemporary economic issues. The editor has asked you to write about the rise of gig economy jobs among young people.
task: Write an article examining the gig economy's impact on young workers in Hong Kong, weighing the flexibility against the lack of job security.
suggestedPoints: Describe the growth of gig economy jobs in Hong Kong
Discuss the appeal of flexibility for young workers
Analyse the drawbacks (lack of benefits, job insecurity)
Compare with traditional employment
Offer a balanced conclusion on the future of work
instructions: Give your article an engaging headline. Write for an educated general audience.
